duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Governor execute() allows execution of Defeated and Expired proposals, completely bypassing governance voting
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the same require statement bug in execute() that permits Defeated/Expired proposal states. ECON-H001 selected for comprehensive attack economics and clear evidence. OPS2-H012 (relay vector) is a specific exploitation path of the same root cause.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GenesisPoolManager.setRouter() has inverted zero-address check (== instead of !=), bricking router updates
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the identical typo require(_router == address(0)) in setRouter(). TECH-H002 has the clearest technical analysis with specific line numbers and impact chain to launch().

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GenesisPoolManager.depositToken() accepts arbitrary unvalidated address as genesisPool, enabling fake pool injection for gauge creation and token whitelisting
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the missing factory validation on the genesisPool parameter in depositToken(), allowing a malicious contract to trigger _preLaunchPool() and create unauthorized gauges. ECON-H002 selected for CRITICAL rating with comprehensive economics and clear exploit path.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Thenian mintPublic() reentrancy via _safeMint callback bypasses per-wallet/per-transaction NFT limits
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same _safeMint reentrancy in mintPublic() allowing bypass of mint limits. TECH-H004 has more detailed exploit mechanics and specific line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Governor.sol _voteSucceeded requires forVotes > abstainVotes, enabling abstain-based proposal blocking or manipulation into Expired state
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the non-standard _voteSucceeded condition requiring forVotes > abstainVotes. ECON-H005 provides the most comprehensive analysis including interaction with the execute() bug and attack economics.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Zero slippage protection (amountAMin=0, amountBMin=0) in addLiquidity during genesis pool launch enables price manipulation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the zero min amounts in IRouter.addLiquidity() call within _addLiquidityAndDistribute(). ECON2-H009 is a specific attack path (pair manipulation between approval and launch) of the same root cause. ECON2-H007 selected for most detailed multi-block attack economics and clear evidence.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Governor.sol quorum() uses current total supply instead of historical snapshot supply, allowing quorum manipulation
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe quorum() calling getsmNFTPastTotalSupply() without the blockTimestamp parameter. TECH-H007 selected for clearest technical evidence including comparison with the correct L2GovernorVotesQuorumFraction implementation.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Incentives mapping not zeroed after transferIncentives() causes irrecoverable claims when pool transitions to NOT_QUALIFIED
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe transferIncentives() sending tokens to external bribe but not zeroing incentives[token], causing claimIncentives() to revert on NOT_QUALIFIED pools. TECH-H008 selected for clearest lifecycle description and specific line references.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Single reverting genesis pool in checkAtEpochFlip/checkBeforeEpochFlip blocks all other pools due to missing try-catch
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the unbounded loop over liveNativeTokens with external calls and no try-catch, where one revert blocks all. ECON-H007 selected for HIGH rating with comprehensive economics including pre_epoch_period stale update detail.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: deductAmount() arithmetic issues (division-by-zero when liquidity is small, underflow in userDeposits subtraction for tokenOwner)
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All target deductAmount() arithmetic: div-by-zero when liquidity/2=0, rounding underflow for tokenOwner-as-depositor, and guaranteed underflow for tokenOwner-without-deposits or non-genesis stakers. ECON2-H003 selected for identifying the most practically impactful scenario (tokenOwner with 0 userDeposits guaranteed to lose LP tokens) with clear economic impact.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Pair pre-funding via dust transfer permanently blocks genesis pool approval due to strict zero-balance check
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe sending 1 wei to the pair address to fail the balanceOf == 0 check in approveGenesisPool/depositNativeToken. ECON-H014 selected for better attack economics and clearer griefing scenario.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: SafeERC20.safeApprove() used without resetting to 0 first can DoS with USDT-style tokens in transferIncentives and _approveTokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe safeApprove(spender, amount) without prior approve(spender, 0), causing revert for tokens with non-zero-to-non-zero allowance restrictions. TECH-H014 selected for covering both transferIncentives AND _approveTokens code paths with HIGH severity.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: balanceOf() division by zero when totalDeposits is zero after launch
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe the same division by totalDeposits in balanceOf() with no zero-check. ECON-H010 selected for more thorough investigation rationale analyzing deductAmount/deductAllAmount interaction with totalDeposits.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Missing on-chain enforcement of NO_GENESIS_DEPOSIT_WINDOW allows late deposits if keeper is delayed
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe depositToken() lacking an upper-bound timestamp check for the deposit window end. ECON-H020 selected for having attack economics quantifying the sniping advantage.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Null auction address from removed AuctionFactory entry bricks genesis pool deposits
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: All describe the auction selection fallback to auctions(0) returning address(0) when index 0 is removed. TECH-H018 selected for most comprehensive coverage of the full chain from AuctionFactory through GenesisPoolManager to GenesisPool.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: Stale liveNativeTokensIndex not deleted on removal can cause wrong token to be removed from tracking array
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe _removeLiveToken not deleting liveNativeTokensIndex for the removed token. ECON2-H013 selected for HIGH severity with more detailed exploitation scenario showing how stale index causes wrong token removal.

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GenesisPool claim functions lack reentrancy guard, enabling cross-function reentrancy with callback tokens
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe missing ReentrancyGuard on GenesisPool claim functions allowing reentrancy via ERC777/callback tokens. ECON2-H014 selected for HIGH severity with clearer cross-function reentrancy analysis (claimNative → claimIncentives).

duplicate_groups.vulnerability_description: GenesisPoolFactory.getGenesisPool() only checks last pool entry, enabling dynamic resolution mismatch and orphaned pools
duplicate_groups.removal_reason: Both describe getGenesisPool only checking pools[length-1], causing either wrong pool to be launched (TECH-H012) or earlier pools to be orphaned (ECON2-H015). TECH-H012 selected for CRITICAL severity with more impactful emissions diversion scenario.
